common_issues: Bolting (heat >28C, long days, water stress); tip burn (Ca translocation failure under high VPD/fast growth); downy mildew (Hyaloperonospora, RH>90%); black rot (Xanthomonas, V-shaped lesions); Alternaria leaf spot; Pythium/Phytophthora root rot (warm reservoir, low DO); Erwinia/Pectobacterium soft rot; damping-off at seedling stage; diamondback moth (windowpane then holes); flea beetle shot-hole; aphids (virus vectors); cabbage looper, webworm, whitefly, thrips, leaf miner; nitrate accumulation; Ca-Mg-K antagonism; Mg deficiency; B deficiency (cracked petioles); Fe lockout from pH drift; algae in channels; heat-induced bitterness; uneven sizing from poor flow; root mat blocking NFT.
harvest_indicator: Ready 25-35 DAT; 8-12 fully expanded true leaves; rosette 20-25cm tall, 18-22cm wide; whole-plant fresh weight 150-300 g; petioles thick, crisp, pale green-white; deep green turgid leaves, no yellowing or tip burn; no flower stalk; harvest 5-7 AM.
tips: Sow 3-5mm deep in pre-soaked rockwool or peat plugs at 22-25C; expect 80-95% germination in 2-4 days under continuous moisture and 50-70% humidity.
Provide seedlings 14-16 hours of light at 150-200 umol/m2/s PPFD starting on day 2 to prevent leggy hypocotyls; keep root-zone temperature at 20-24C.
Begin nutrient feeding at EC 0.6-0.8 mS/cm (pH 5.8-6.2) once cotyledons fully open, then ramp to EC 1.2 by transplant to harden tissue against tip burn.
Transplant at 14-18 days when 2-3 true leaves have formed and roots protrude 2-3cm from the plug; spacing 15-20cm in NFT/DFT channels (about 25-30 plants/m2).
Maintain vegetative EC 1.4-1.8 mS/cm with a Ca:K ratio near 1:1.2 and supplemental Ca(NO3)2 at 150-180 ppm Ca to suppress inner-leaf tip burn common in PH lowland heat.
Hold dissolved oxygen above 6 mg/L and reservoir temperature below 26C; use chillers, shaded reservoirs, or night-only top-ups to prevent Pythium.
Target air temperature 18-24C day / 15-20C night; sustained >28C triggers bolting and bitter flavor, so deploy 30-50% shade net and evaporative cooling during dry season (March-May).
Run airflow at 0.3-0.5 m/s across the canopy with VPD 0.8-1.2 kPa to dampen downy mildew and improve calcium transpiration flow.

Scout twice weekly for diamondback moth (Plutella xylostella), flea beetle, and aphids; deploy yellow sticky traps at canopy height and rotate Bacillus thuringiensis kurstaki with spinosad to delay resistance.
Flush the system with plain pH-adjusted water (EC <= 0.4) for 24-48 hours before harvest to reduce nitrate accumulation, which is high in fast-grown Brassicas.
Harvest whole plant at 25-35 DAT in early morning (5-7 AM) when leaves are turgid and field heat is lowest; cut at the crown with sanitized blades to avoid Erwinia soft rot entry.
Pre-cool harvested heads to 2-4C within 1 hour and store at 0-2C with 95-98% RH for 14-21 days shelf life; never store with ethylene producers (tomato, banana) to avoid yellowing.

risks.title: Premature bolting
risks.description: Sustained temperatures above 28C combined with long photoperiods or moisture stress trigger reproductive transition, causing elongated stems, small bitter leaves, and flower stalk emergence. Symptoms include sudden vertical stem extension, narrowing of new leaves, and loss of crisp midrib texture.
risks.mitigation: Schedule cropping cycles to avoid March-May peak heat or use 30-50% shade net plus reservoir chilling to keep root-zone <= 24C. Select bolt-resistant cultivars such as Black Behi, Hybrid Black Summer, or Joi Choi F1 bred for tropical lowland conditions.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Localized calcium deficiency in rapidly expanding inner leaves causes necrotic browning along leaf margins even when nutrient solution Ca is adequate, because transpiration cannot deliver Ca to enclosed tissues. It typically appears 7-14 days before harvest under high light, low humidity, or fast EC growth.
risks.mitigation: Maintain 150-180 ppm Ca, keep VPD 0.8-1.2 kPa, and run gentle horizontal airflow across the inner canopy to drive Ca-bearing transpiration. Apply foliar 0.3% CaCl2 weekly during the last 14 days and avoid EC spikes >2.0.

risks.title: Downy mildew (Hyaloperonospora parasitica)
risks.description: Cool, humid mornings (>90% RH for >6 hr) allow oomycete sporulation, producing yellow angular lesions on the upper leaf surface with grey-violet sporangia underneath. Severe infections cause leaf collapse and unmarketable heads within 5-7 days.
risks.mitigation: Maintain RH 60-75%, increase canopy airflow to 0.3-0.5 m/s, and remove infected leaves immediately into sealed bags. Rotate preventive sprays of copper hydroxide (1.5 g/L) with Bacillus subtilis QST 713 or potassium phosphonate at 7-day intervals during wet season.

risks.title: Diamondback moth and flea beetle
risks.description: Plutella xylostella larvae create characteristic windowpane feeding then irregular holes, while flea beetles produce shot-hole damage on young leaves, both reducing marketable yield drastically. Populations explode in dry, warm Philippine conditions with overlapping cabbage crops nearby.
risks.mitigation: Enclose systems with 32-mesh insect netting, deploy yellow sticky traps at 1 per 4 m2, and rotate Bt kurstaki (1 g/L), spinosad (0.4 ml/L), and emamectin benzoate weekly to prevent resistance. Maintain a sanitation buffer free of cruciferous weeds (mustard, radish, wild bok choy) within 10 m.

risks.title: Pythium root rot
risks.description: Pythium aphanidermatum thrives when reservoir temperature exceeds 26C and DO drops below 5 mg/L, attacking root tips and producing brown, slimy, sloughing roots with rapid above-ground wilting under high light. Plants collapse within 48-72 hours of symptom onset.
risks.mitigation: Chill nutrient solution to 20-24C, maintain DO >= 6 mg/L via air stones or venturi injection, and dose hydrogen peroxide at 30-50 ppm or ionic copper-silver as a preventive. Sanitize channels between cycles with 1-2% sodium hypochlorite followed by thorough rinse.

risks.title: Nutrient imbalance (N excess and Ca-Mg antagonism)
risks.description: Over-reliance on ammoniacal N or excess K suppresses Ca and Mg uptake, causing interveinal chlorosis on older leaves, soft floppy growth, and elevated nitrate residues exceeding food-safety thresholds. Misreadings occur because EC remains in range while individual ion ratios drift.
risks.mitigation: Use >= 80% nitrate-N formulation, maintain K:Ca:Mg molar ratio near 1.2:1:0.4, and refresh reservoir fully every 10-14 days rather than topping up indefinitely. Run lab or ion-selective testing monthly and adjust stock A/B ratios accordingly.

risks.title: Heat stress and wilting
risks.description: Midday canopy temperature >32C with PPFD >500 umol/m2/s causes stomatal closure, transpiration collapse, and visible flagging of outer leaves; chronic exposure reduces head weight by 30-50%. Flavor turns bitter due to glucosinolate accumulation.
risks.mitigation: Install 30-50% white shade net, mist intermittently to drop leaf temperature 3-5C, and time peak growth periods to cooler months (November-February in Luzon lowlands). Elevate growing surfaces and insulate reservoirs to limit conductive heat gain.

growth_stages.description: Seeds absorb water, activate amylases, and rupture the seed coat as the radicle emerges. The hypocotyl hook begins to lift cotyledons; uniform moisture and temperature are critical to synchronize the cohort.
actions: Pre-soak rockwool/peat plugs in pH 5.8 water (EC 0.4)
Keep in darkness or low light <50 umol/m2/s
Avoid any nutrient solution until cotyledons emerge
Inspect daily for damping-off; remove failed plugs

growth_stages.description: Cotyledons fully open and turn green as photosynthesis begins; primary root extends 2-4cm. Seedlings are highly sensitive to overwatering and low light, which causes etiolation.
actions: Begin 14-16 h photoperiod at 150-200 umol/m2/s PPFD
Reduce humidity to 65-75% by venting dome
Start nutrient feed at EC 0.6-0.8, pH 5.8-6.2
Provide gentle airflow (0.2 m/s) to strengthen stems
Thin to 1 seedling per plug if doubled

growth_stages.description: First and second true leaves expand and the plant transitions to autotrophic growth. Root system branches out of the plug; stem thickens in preparation for transplant.
actions: Harden seedlings with airflow 0.3 m/s for 2 days pre-transplant
Scout for early aphid/flea beetle activity

growth_stages.description: Seedlings move into NFT/DFT channels or Kratky containers; roots adjust to flowing solution and new pH/EC environment. Brief transplant shock may cause leaf droop for 24-48 hours.
actions: Transplant at 2-3 true leaf stage, spacing 15-20cm
Set NFT flow 1-2 L/min per channel; DFT depth 3-5cm
Verify all roots contact solution film

growth_stages.description: Rapid leaf production and biomass accumulation; rosette diameter doubles weekly under good conditions. Highest nutrient and water demand of the cycle.
actions: Hold VPD 0.8-1.2 kPa for steady transpiration
Foliar Ca (0.3% CaCl2) weekly to prevent tip burn
Begin biweekly Bt or spinosad rotations for DBM

growth_stages.description: Inner leaves cup and thicken the petiole bases; fresh weight accumulates fastest in this window. Calcium translocation to inner leaves is the main physiological bottleneck.
actions: Keep canopy temp <= 26C; deploy shade if needed
Sustain horizontal airflow over inner canopy
Remove any necrotic outer leaves promptly
Final pest scout 5 days before harvest

growth_stages.description: Plant reaches marketable size (150-300 g, 20-25cm tall); nitrate flushing improves food safety and shelf life. Tissue firms slightly as growth slows.
actions: Switch to plain pH-adjusted water (EC <= 0.4) for 24-48 h
Maintain light and airflow as before
Inspect for tip burn, pest damage, soft rot
Pre-cool harvest tools and crates to 4C

growth_stages.description: Whole-plant cut at the crown; rapid field heat removal preserves turgor and chlorophyll. Tissue continues respiring, so cold chain integrity determines shelf life.
actions: Cut at crown with sanitized stainless blade
Pre-cool to 2-4C within 1 hour (hydrocool or forced air)
Pack in vented crates, max 2-3 layers
Target market within 7-14 days for premium quality

common_issues: Tip burn: low Ca translocation from high RH/low airflow/high EC. Bolting: temp >24C, long days, water stress. Bitterness: heat stress, over-maturity, EC spikes. Bottom rot (Sclerotinia/Rhizoctonia): wet crowns, dense canopy. Downy mildew (Bremia): cool wet nights, RH>90%, leaf wetness >4h. Pythium root rot: solution >24C, DO<5 mg/L. Fusarium wilt race 1: warm root zone, infected seed. Damping-off: wet plugs, cold media. Aphids (Nasonovia) hidden in heads. Leafminer (Liriomyza) serpentine mines. Thrips silvering and virus vector. Whitefly with sooty mold. Fungus gnats chewing roots. Spider mites stippling. Slugs ragged holes. LTBV/LMV viruses aphid/seed-borne mottling. N=pale older leaves; Ca=tip burn; Mg=interveinal old leaves; Fe=interveinal new leaves at pH>6.3; B=cracked midribs/hollow stem; Mo=marginal scorch. EC drift, pH crash from nitrification, algae in channels, root browning from low DO.
harvest_indicator: Ready 28-40 DAT (42-55 DAS); head 20-30 cm tall, base 10-15 cm; whole-head 250-450 g; 18-26 leaves with 6-10 cupped inner leaves; gentle squeeze yields slight resistance; no seed-stalk at core; no bitterness in midrib; cut 5-7 AM; cool to 1-2C within 30 min.
tips: Pre-chill romaine seed at 4-5C for 24-48 hours before sowing to break thermo-dormancy, since germination drops sharply above 25C media temperature.
Sow pelleted seed into pre-soaked phenolic foam or rockwool plugs at pH 5.5-5.8, hold media at 18-22C, and germinate under low light (100-150 umol/m2/s) until cotyledons fully expand on day 2-3.
Run seedlings at EC 0.8-1.2 mS/cm for the first 10-14 days, then transplant to NFT/DFT channels at the 3-4 true-leaf stage (day 14-18) when roots emerge cleanly from the plug bottom.
Ramp nutrient EC stepwise: 1.2 at transplant, 1.6 by week 2 in channels, and 1.8-2.2 mS/cm during head-fill, never exceeding 2.4 in PH lowland heat to avoid osmotic-driven tip burn.
Hold solution pH at 5.6-6.0 (target 5.8); romaine Ca and Mg uptake drops sharply above 6.3 and Fe/Mn lock out below 5.4.
Keep root-zone solution temperature at 18-22C using a chiller or insulated/shaded reservoir; above 25C dissolved oxygen falls below 6 mg/L and Pythium pressure rises sharply.
Target a Daily Light Integral of 14-17 mol/m2/day (roughly 250-350 umol/m2/s for 14-16 h); under PH midday sun use 40-50% shade cloth to cap PPFD near 600 umol/m2/s and prevent bolting.
Maintain canopy air at 18-24C day / 15-18C night with VPD 0.8-1.2 kPa and continuous gentle airflow (0.3-0.5 m/s) to drive Ca-bearing transpiration into inner heads.

For PH lowland conditions choose bolt- and heat-tolerant cos cultivars: Jericho (Israeli desert-bred), Coastal Star, Sun Valley, and Parris Island Cos; avoid butterhead-leaning romaines below 800 m elevation.
Pre-harvest cool by dropping reservoir EC to 1.4 mS/cm and lowering canopy temperature (early-AM harvest) to raise leaf turgor, sugar, and shelf life.
Harvest at 28-40 days after transplant (42-55 DAS) when heads reach 250-450 g and inner leaves are firm but not yet elongating into a seed stalk; cut whole-head at the base with a clean blade.
Post-harvest, hydro-cool or force-air cool to 1-2C within 30 minutes, store at 0-2C and 95-98% RH; romaine holds 14-21 days when cooled fast, under 5 days if field heat is retained.

risks.title: Tip burn (Ca translocation failure)
risks.description: Necrotic margins on the youngest inner leaves caused by localized Ca deficiency in fast-expanding tissue, not by solution Ca shortage. It is driven by low transpiration into the inner head (high humidity, still air, dark interiors) and by rapid growth from high EC, high N, or high light.
risks.mitigation: Maintain VPD 0.8-1.2 kPa with horizontal airflow fans directed across the canopy, cap EC at 1.8-2.0 during head-fill, and ensure Ca at 160-200 ppm with a Ca:K ratio near 1:1.2. In high-risk weeks, run a small overhead fan blowing into the head center for 2-3 hours midday to force inner-leaf transpiration.

risks.title: Bolting / premature seed stalk
risks.description: Romaine bolts when mean daily temperature exceeds 24C for several days, especially under long photoperiods and water stress, producing bitter latex and elongated cores. Once the apical meristem switches to reproductive growth the crop is unsalvageable.
risks.mitigation: Use bolt-tolerant cultivars (Jericho, Coastal Star, Sun Valley), keep reservoir at 18-22C and canopy under 26C with shade cloth, and shorten cycle to 28-32 DAT in hot months. Avoid EC swings and never let the channel run dry.

risks.title: Bottom rot (Sclerotinia / Rhizoctonia)
risks.description: Water-soaked brown lesions on the lower leaves and crown, often with white cottony mycelium and black sclerotia, collapsing the head from below. Favored by dense canopies, prolonged leaf wetness, and contact between lower leaves and wet media or channels.
risks.mitigation: Maintain channel slope (1:30-1:50) so no standing water pools at the crown, space heads at 18-22 cm to keep canopy open, and remove any infected plant plus surrounding plugs immediately. Sanitize channels between cycles with 1-2% hydrogen peroxide or 200 ppm peracetic acid.

risks.title: Downy mildew (Bremia lactucae)
risks.description: Pale yellow angular spots on upper leaf surfaces with white-to-grey downy sporulation underneath, spreading rapidly in cool nights with leaf wetness above 4 hours. Spores are airborne and races shift, defeating single-gene resistance quickly.
risks.mitigation: Grow Bremia-resistant cultivars carrying current Bl IR genes (check seed catalog for Bl 16-37 coverage), keep RH below 85% at night with ventilation or dehumidification, and avoid overhead irrigation. Preventive sprays of Bacillus subtilis QST 713 or potassium bicarbonate weekly during cool, wet weeks.

risks.title: Pythium root rot (P. aphanidermatum, P. dissotocum)
risks.description: Brown, sloughing roots with loss of root cortex, wilting at midday despite full reservoirs, and stunted heads. Triggered by warm solution (above 24C), low dissolved oxygen (under 5 mg/L), and organic debris in the system.
risks.mitigation: Chill solution to 18-22C, maintain DO above 6 mg/L with air stones or venturi injectors, and dose 30-50 ppm hydrogen peroxide or 0.5-1.0 ppm ozone-equivalent continuously. Filter return flow through 100-mesh and flush channels between crops.

risks.title: Aphids and leafminers (Nasonovia, Liriomyza)
risks.description: Aphids colonize the head interior where they are unreachable by sprays and contaminate harvested product, while Liriomyza larvae mine serpentine tunnels through leaves rendering them unmarketable. Both vector secondary problems including viruses and sooty mold.
risks.mitigation: Screen greenhouse intakes with 50-mesh insect netting, scout with yellow sticky traps weekly, and release Aphidius colemani at 0.5-1 per m2 at first detection. For leafminer use Diglyphus isaea or rotate spinosad and abamectin observing PHI.

risks.title: Bitterness from heat or over-maturity
risks.description: Romaine accumulates sesquiterpene lactones (lactucin, lactucopicrin) in the latex when stressed by heat above 28C, water deficit, or harvest past physiological maturity. Bitterness is irreversible after harvest and downgrades the crop.
risks.mitigation: Harvest within 3-5 days of reaching target weight, cut in the cool early morning, and avoid letting EC spike or reservoirs warm during the final week. Maintain steady irrigation and shade during heat waves above 30C ambient.

growth_stages.description: Seed takes up water and gibberellin signaling triggers radicle emergence; romaine germinates best at 18-22C and is thermo-inhibited above 25C. Look for radicle protrusion by 36-48 hours and cotyledon greening by day 3.
actions: Pre-soak rockwool/phenolic plugs in pH 5.8, EC 0.5 mS/cm Ca-nitrate solution for 1 hour
Sow pelleted seed 3-4 mm deep, one per cell, and cover tray with humidity dome
Hold media at 18-22C; pre-chill seed at 5C for 24 h if ambient is hot
Keep RH at 90-95% until radicle visible
Provide 100-150 umol/m2/s light or diffuse daylight from day 1 to prevent stretch
Do not feed beyond pre-soak nutrients during this stage

growth_stages.description: Cotyledons fully expand and the first true leaf initiates as the seedling shifts from seed reserves to autotrophic growth. Watch for uniform greening, straight hypocotyls, and roots reaching the lower third of the plug.
actions: Remove humidity dome at day 3-4 to harden seedlings
Begin feeding at EC 0.8 mS/cm, pH 5.6-5.8 with full lettuce formulation (N 150, K 200, Ca 160, Mg 45 ppm)
Water from below to keep cotyledons dry and prevent damping-off
Maintain airflow with a small fan to thicken stems

growth_stages.description: Root system colonizes the plug and leaf area expands rapidly; this is the photosynthetic ramp-up phase. Inspect for white healthy roots emerging at the plug base and reject any leggy or yellow seedlings.
actions: Raise EC to 1.0-1.2 mS/cm, hold pH 5.8
Increase PPFD to 200-250 umol/m2/s, DLI 12-14 mol/m2/day
Apply preventive Bacillus subtilis or Trichoderma drench to plug
Grade seedlings; cull runts before transplant
Begin scouting for fungus gnats and aphids with sticky cards

growth_stages.description: Roots transition from plug to channel solution; the plant briefly slows growth while new roots adapt to flowing nutrient film or static aerated reservoir. Watch for root extension into the channel within 48-72 hours and erect new leaves emerging from the crown.
actions: Transplant at 3-4 true leaves with roots clearly emerging from plug
Channel EC 1.2-1.4 mS/cm, pH 5.8, solution temp 18-22C
NFT flow 1-2 L/min per channel, film 2-3 mm; DFT depth 3-5 cm with continuous aeration; Kratky leave 2-3 cm air gap below netcup
Spacing 18-22 cm between plants, 25-30 cm rows
Maintain DO above 6 mg/L; air-stone DFT/Kratky reservoirs
Shade newly transplanted seedlings 30% for first 2 days
Verify channel slope and no pooling at plant base

growth_stages.description: Rapid leaf production and rosette diameter expansion; canopy closes between plants and biomass accumulation peaks. Look for symmetric rosette, deep green color, and 8-14 expanded leaves.
actions: DLI 14-17 mol/m2/day; cap PPFD at 500-600 umol/m2/s with 40-50% shade in PH midday
Run horizontal airflow fans 0.3-0.5 m/s over canopy to reduce tip burn
Top up reservoir daily; full nutrient change every 7-10 days or when EC drift exceeds 0.4
Scout twice weekly for Bremia, aphids, and leafminers
Maintain DO above 6 mg/L and root-zone temp under 22C

growth_stages.description: Inner leaves begin to fold upright and form the characteristic upright cos head; this is the highest tip-burn-risk window. Look for tightening of central leaves and base diameter reaching 10-14 cm.
actions: Hold EC 1.8-2.0 mS/cm; do not exceed 2.2 in hot weeks
Maintain Ca 160-200 ppm, Ca:K ratio near 1:1.2, B 0.3-0.5 ppm
Direct gentle airflow into head centers 2-3 h midday to force inner-leaf transpiration
Keep night RH below 85% to limit Bremia and Sclerotinia
Remove any lower leaves touching channel surface

growth_stages.description: Head reaches target size and dry matter, sugars accumulate, and any further growth risks bolting or bitterness. Check head firmness with light hand pressure and look for tight, upright inner leaves with no seed-stalk elongation.
actions: Drop EC to 1.4-1.6 mS/cm for 3-5 days to reduce nitrate and improve flavor
Shade to 50% and target canopy under 24C
Withhold any pesticide within label PHI
Confirm head weight 250-450 g and base diameter 10-15 cm
Schedule harvest at 5-7 AM when leaves are most turgid
Sanitize knives and harvest crates with 200 ppm chlorine or peroxyacetic acid

growth_stages.description: Whole-head cut at the base and immediate cooling locks in quality; field heat above 20C halves shelf life. Look for crisp upright leaves, no slimy base, and no latex browning at the cut.
actions: Cut at the crown with a single clean stroke; trim 1-2 outer wrapper leaves
Hydro-cool or force-air cool to 1-2C within 30 minutes
Pack in vented crates; avoid stacking pressure on heads
Target shelf life 14-21 days when cold chain is maintained
Sanitize and flush channels with 1-2% H2O2 before next cycle

common_issues: White rust (Albugo) white pustules on leaf underside; airflow + copper/metalaxyl. Leaf miner serpentine tunnels; spinosad, sticky traps, 32-mesh net. Flea beetle shotholes; neem + insect net. Pythium root rot from warm stagnant solution; airstones, nutrient temp <28C, H2O2 30 ppm. Hollow pithy stem from late harvest or low boron; cut by day 28, hold B 0.3-0.5 ppm. Leggy etiolated growth under low light; DLI >= 12, thin to 25-35 plants/m2. N-deficient yellowing of older leaves after ratoon; top up Ca(NO3)2+KNO3, NO3-N >= 180 ppm. Bolting in long photoperiod or heat; harvest before bud, shade 30% in dry season. Fe/Mn lockout above pH 6.8; daily pH check. Tip burn from Ca deficiency under high EC and low transpiration; Ca 150-180 ppm with airflow. Aphid/whitefly clusters on shoot tips; yellow traps, insecticidal soap. Damping-off at germination from saturated cool plugs.
harvest_indicator: First cut 28-30 days from sow (24-26 from transplant); ratoon cuts every 14-21 days. Stems 25-35 cm with 6+ internodes, diameter 4-6 mm, before flower buds. Cut 3-5 cm above crown leaving 2 nodes. Harvest 5-8 AM for max turgor. Plant yield 80-150 g per cut.
tips: Lightly nick or rub seeds against 220-grit sandpaper, then soak in 30-35C clean water for 18-24 hours (change water at 12 h) to lift germination from ~50% to >85%.
Hold media (rockwool/coco/peat plug) at 25-32C with constant moisture; radicle emerges in 2-4 days, true leaves by day 7. Below 22C germination stalls; above 35C causes seedling damping-off.
Use the upland/dryland type (narrow-leaf, erect) for NFT, DFT, and Kratky; reserve the aquatic/broadleaf type for deep-water raft systems where stems can sprawl on the water surface.

Practice cut-and-come-again: 4-6 successive cuts at 14-21 day intervals from one sowing, cutting 3-5 cm above the crown leaving 2 nodes for regrowth. Total productive cycle 60-90 days before lignification forces replant.
EC ramp: seedlings 0.6-0.8, vegetative 1.4-1.8, pre-harvest 1.6-2.0 mS/cm; after each ratoon cut drop EC by 0.2 for 48 h to favor soft regrowth, then ramp back. Keep N:K near 1.5:1.
Maintain pH 5.8-6.5; kangkong tolerates slight alkalinity (up to 7.0) better than lettuce but Fe/Mn lockout begins above 6.8. Check pH twice daily in shallow Kratky reservoirs.

Ratooning protocol: after cutting, immediately top up reservoir, foliar-mist Ca(NO3)2 at 0.5 g/L, and resume full EC within 24 h. Each successive ratoon yields ~80% of the previous; cull when stems exceed 8 mm or turn hollow.
Sow-to-first-harvest in PH lowland conditions: 24-30 days for upland type, 28-35 days for aquatic. Stagger weekly sowings for continuous supply.
Thrives at 25-35C ambient and tolerates 38C with adequate root-zone cooling (<28C nutrient temp); above 32C nutrient temp, DO drops below 5 mg/L, so add airstones or shade reservoir with 30% net.

Propagate by stem cuttings: take 15-20 cm cuttings with 3-4 nodes, strip lower leaves, direct-stick into net pots with rockwool collar; roots emerge in 4-6 days at 28C, shaving 10-14 days off cycle (dominant PH commercial practice).
Provide DLI >= 12 mol/m2/day; under PH dry-season sun (March-May) use 30% shade cloth to prevent leaf scorch and bolting. Indoor LED 200-250 umol/m2/s for 14-16 h photoperiod.

Scout twice weekly for leaf miner (Liriomyza), flea beetle, aphids, and whitefly; deploy yellow sticky traps and rotate neem oil 0.5%, spinosad, and Bt for lepidopteran larvae. Avoid systemic neonics within 14 days of harvest.

risks.title: White rust (Albugo ipomoeae)
risks.description: Chalky white blister-like pustules on leaf undersides with chlorotic yellow spots above; spreads rapidly under high humidity (>85%) and leaf wetness. Severe infection defoliates plants within 7-10 days and renders the crop unmarketable.
risks.mitigation: Increase canopy airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, and rogue infected leaves immediately into sealed bags. Apply copper hydroxide (2 g/L) or metalaxyl-mancozeb at first symptom; rotate kangkong out of affected channels for 2 cycles.

risks.title: Leaf miner and flea beetle damage
risks.description: Liriomyza larvae tunnel serpentine mines through leaf mesophyll while flea beetles create characteristic shothole feeding on tender leaves. Both reduce photosynthetic area and grade-out marketable leaves at retail.
risks.mitigation: Deploy yellow sticky traps at 1 trap per 4 m2 and cover seedling stages with 32-mesh insect net. Spray spinosad (0.4 mL/L) or abamectin on rotation; release Diglyphus isaea parasitoid in protected houses.

risks.title: Root rot in stagnant water (Pythium / Phytophthora)
risks.description: Brown, slimy, sloughing roots and sudden midday wilt despite full reservoirs, driven by nutrient temps >30C and DO <4 mg/L. Aquatic-type kangkong tolerates low O2 better than upland-type, but both succumb in stagnant warm Kratky setups.
risks.mitigation: Add airstones (>= 0.5 L/min per 10 L reservoir), shade or insulate tanks to keep nutrient solution <28C, and dose hydrogen peroxide at 30 ppm weekly or beneficial Trichoderma harzianum.

risks.title: Low light leading to leggy etiolated growth
risks.description: Under <10 mol/m2/day DLI or dense planting (>40 plants/m2), internodes elongate to 8-12 cm, stems become pale and weak, and bundles fail size grading. Common in roofed wet-season nurseries and over-shaded balconies.
risks.mitigation: Maintain DLI >= 12, thin to 25-35 plants/m2, and supplement with 150 umol/m2/s LED for 4 h dawn extension. Pinch growing tips at 4-leaf stage to force lateral branching.

risks.title: Hollow-stem disorder and lignification
risks.description: Late-cut or boron-deficient plants develop hollow, pithy main stems and tough fibrous bases that consumers reject. Often paired with cracked petioles and necrotic shoot tips.
risks.mitigation: Harvest by day 28-30 from sow (or day 18-21 from previous cut) before stem diameter exceeds 6 mm. Maintain B at 0.3-0.5 ppm and Ca at 150-180 ppm in solution.

risks.title: Nitrogen deficiency yellowing
risks.description: Uniform yellowing of older leaves progressing upward with reduced leaf size and stunted regrowth after ratooning; kangkong is a heavy N feeder (NO3-N demand 180-220 ppm). Common after the 2nd or 3rd cut when reservoir N is depleted.
risks.mitigation: Top up calcium nitrate plus potassium nitrate immediately after each cut, targeting NO3 >= 180 ppm. Monitor EC drift and do a full nutrient refresh every 14 days or after 3 ratoons.

risks.title: Regulated invasive species status
risks.description: Ipomoea aquatica is a federally listed noxious weed in the USA and restricted in several jurisdictions because escaped fragments root and clog waterways. In the Philippines it is native and unrestricted, but growers exporting transplants or operating near protected wetlands must contain runoff.
risks.mitigation: Filter all reservoir discharge through 1 mm screen and compost trimmings on-site; never dump nutrient solution containing stem fragments into canals or irrigation ditches. Confirm local DA/DENR rules before establishing systems near Laguna de Bay or other RAMSAR sites.

growth_stages.description: Hard-coated seeds are scarified and pre-soaked to break dormancy and synchronize germination. Without this step, germination drops below 50% and emergence is staggered over 2 weeks.
actions: Lightly abrade seeds with 220-grit sandpaper or nick coat with nail clipper
Soak in 30-35C clean water for 18-24 hours; change water at 12 h
Keep soaking vessel in shade at 28-30C ambient
Drain and surface-dry 30 min before sowing

growth_stages.description: Radicle emerges from scarified seed under warm, humid conditions in rockwool or coco plugs. Cotyledons unfold by day 4; this stage is most vulnerable to damping-off.
actions: Sow 1-2 seeds per plug at 5 mm depth
Maintain media temp 25-32C; humidity 80-90% under dome
Keep media moist with pH 5.8-6.2 plain water (no nutrients yet)
Provide low light 50-100 umol/m2/s once radicle visible
EC 0 (plain water) or 0.4 mS/cm dilute starter
Ventilate dome 10 min daily to prevent fungal bloom
Remove dome at full cotyledon expansion

growth_stages.description: First and second true leaves expand and the taproot extends into the plug. Plant becomes light-hungry and begins active nutrient uptake.
actions: Increase light to 150-200 umol/m2/s, 14 h photoperiod
Begin nutrient feed at EC 0.6-0.8 mS/cm, pH 5.8-6.2
Thin to 1 strongest seedling per plug

growth_stages.description: Plugs with 2-3 true leaves and visible roots are moved into NFT channels, DFT rafts, or Kratky pots. Spacing set for final canopy.
actions: Transplant when roots protrude >= 1 cm from plug
Shade 30% for 48 h post-transplant to reduce wilt
Verify net pot contact with nutrient film/water surface
Inspect for damping-off; remove failed plugs

growth_stages.description: Rapid stem elongation and leaf production; plant adds 2-3 nodes per week. Highest nutrient demand period; N and K uptake peaks.
actions: DLI 12-16 mol/m2/day; supplement LED if cloudy
Scout weekly for leaf miner, flea beetle, aphids
Top off reservoir daily; full refresh every 10-14 days
Pinch tip at 6-leaf stage to force lateral branching (optional)

growth_stages.description: Stems reach 25-35 cm and leaves attain full size and color. Sugar and dry matter accumulate; pre-flowering stage with no buds yet.
actions: Reduce nutrient temp toward 24-26C to firm tissue
Withhold any spray with PHI >3 days
Inspect for white rust pustules on leaf undersides
Confirm internode count >= 6, stem diameter 4-6 mm
Plan harvest in early AM (5-8 AM) for max turgor

growth_stages.description: Cut stems 3-5 cm above crown leaving 2 basal nodes for regrowth. Yields 80-150 g per plant; bundle and cool immediately to 4-10C.
actions: Harvest 5-8 AM before solar heat load
Use sanitized shears; cut above 2nd node from base
Bundle 250-500 g; rinse in 5 ppm chlorinated water
Cool to 4-10C within 2 h of cut
Top up reservoir; foliar mist Ca(NO3)2 at 0.5 g/L
Drop EC to 1.2 mS/cm for 48 h to favor soft regrowth
Inspect cut surfaces; remove any rotted basal stems

growth_stages.description: Lateral shoots emerge from retained nodes within 3-5 days; second cut ready in 14-21 days. Repeat for 4-6 cycles before lignification forces replant.
actions: Ramp EC back to 1.6-1.8 mS/cm within 72 h of cut
Maintain pH 5.8-6.5; refresh reservoir every 14 days
Watch for hollow-stem at base; replant if stem >8 mm
Cull plants showing white rust or root rot immediately
Track yield per cut; replant when output drops <60% of cut 1
Sanitize channels between full crop cycles

common_issues: Fusarium wilt; flowering reduces flavor
tips: Pinch flowering tips weekly to keep oils concentrated in leaves.
Propagate easily — root 10cm cuttings in plain water within 7 days.

risks.title: Fusarium wilt
risks.description: Sudden one-sided wilting and brown vascular streaks.
risks.mitigation: Use clean rockwool, sanitize reservoir, avoid water above 26C, rotate cultivars.

risks.title: Flowering / loss of flavor
risks.description: Flower spikes redirect energy from leaves.
risks.mitigation: Pinch flower buds twice a week; harvest stem tips regularly.

common_issues: BER (Ca), blight, whitefly
tips: Hand-pollinate by gently shaking trusses each morning.
Prune to 1-2 leaders for Dutch Bucket; remove suckers weekly.
Drop EC 0.3 during fruit ripening to boost flavor.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot (BER)
risks.description: Sunken black patch at fruit base from calcium starvation.
risks.mitigation: Keep EC stable, supplement Cal-Mag, maintain even watering, humidity above 60%.

risks.title: Whitefly infestation
risks.description: Sap-sucking insects on leaf undersides; vector for viruses.
risks.mitigation: Yellow sticky traps; release Encarsia formosa; spray insecticidal soap.

risks.title: Late blight
risks.description: Brown lesions on leaves and fruit during humid spells.
risks.mitigation: Improve airflow, prune lower leaves, copper spray prophylactic.

common_issues: Tip burn at high EC
tips: Avoid overcrowding to prevent leggy growth and shading.
Keep relative humidity around 50-70% to avoid tip burn.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Browning on edges of young leaves due to calcium deficiency.
risks.mitigation: Increase airflow and keep temperature below 24C to aid transpiration.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Premature flowering making leaves bitter.
risks.mitigation: Keep daytime temperatures between 18-24C.

growth_stages.description: Sow in damp sponges or rockwool.

common_issues: Flea beetles; bolting
tips: Cut yellow-flowering stems immediately to delay bolting.
Can use cut-and-come-again harvesting by removing outer 30% of leaves.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Premature flowering and bitter taste from heat stress.
risks.mitigation: Keep temperatures below 26C and monitor system pumps.

common_issues: BER; aphids; slow early growth
tips: Allow first king flower to set then prune subsequent buds for stronger plant.
Trellis with Florida weave to support heavy fruit load.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot
risks.description: Black sunken spot opposite stem from Ca uptake failure.
risks.mitigation: Stable EC, weekly Cal-Mag, humidity 60-75%.

common_issues: Powdery mildew; spider mites
tips: Choose parthenocarpic varieties — no pollination needed.
Train to single-stem umbrella system for max yield per sqm.

risks.title: Calcium/Potassium deficiency
risks.description: Scorched leaf edges or poor fruit set.
risks.mitigation: Supplement calcium and maintain proper EC during fruiting.

risks.title: Powdery mildew
risks.description: White powdery spots on leaves.
risks.mitigation: Ensure good airflow and maintain humidity below 80%.

common_issues: Root rot; poor fruit set in heat
tips: Pinch off early flowers during vegetative stage to build stronger plants.
Use day-neutral varieties like Albion or Seascape for year-round yield.
Requires manual pollination by shaking or using a soft brush.

risks.title: Tip Burn
risks.description: Calcium deficiency from low humidity.
risks.mitigation: Keep humidity above 60%.

risks.title: Root Rot
risks.description: Browning roots due to poor oxygenation.
risks.mitigation: Use air stones and maintain water temp below 20C.

common_issues: Pythium root rot; sensitive to high temperature
tips: Keep nutrient solution cool (under 22C) to prevent Pythium root rot.
Requires cooler ambient temperature to prevent early bolting.

risks.title: Pythium root rot
risks.description: Brown slimy roots in warm water.
risks.mitigation: Keep reservoir cool, add hydrogen peroxide if needed.

common_issues: Invasive root systems in shared channels
tips: Extremely fast growing and roots can easily clog NFT channels if not pruned.
Harvest regularly to promote bushier growth.

risks.title: System clogging
risks.description: Mint roots grow aggressively and can block water flow.
risks.mitigation: Regularly trim roots or grow in individual Dutch buckets.

actions: Place in water until roots form

common_issues: Aphids; high temperatures cause bitterness
harvest_indicator: Leaves are large enough to eat
tips: Use 'cut-and-come-again' harvesting.
Tolerates wider EC range.

risks.title: Aphids
risks.description: Small insects on underside of leaves.
risks.mitigation: Use sticky traps or neem oil.

common_issues: Bolting in high heat
tips: Sensitive to high light/heat causing bolting.
Harvest whole head.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Premature flowering.
risks.mitigation: Keep temps below 24C.

common_issues: Nutrient burn if EC too high
tips: Harvests very quickly in 3-4 weeks.
Sensitive to high salt concentrations.

risks.title: Nutrient Burn
risks.description: Yellowing/browning leaf tips.
risks.mitigation: Keep EC below 1.4.

common_issues: Early bolting; slow germination
tips: Crack seeds gently before planting.
Use slow-bolt varieties.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Flowers early in heat.
risks.mitigation: Keep temperatures cool.

common_issues: Root rot from stagnant water
tips: Hardy and compact.
Propagate from cuttings for faster results.

risks.title: Root Rot
risks.description: Roots drown in stagnant water.
risks.mitigation: Ensure good aeration.

common_issues: Root rot
tips: Prune regularly to prevent woody stems.
Harvest before flowers open.

risks.title: Root Rot
risks.description: Soggy roots.
risks.mitigation: Avoid constant soaking if possible.

common_issues: Overcrowding in net cups
tips: Trim back to 2-3cm above crown every few weeks.
Very productive and easy.

risks.title: Overcrowding
risks.description: Clumps grow too thick.
risks.mitigation: Divide mature clumps occasionally.

common_issues: Spider mites; heavy fruit breaking branches
tips: Requires robust trellising.
Manual pollination needed indoors.

risks.title: Branch Breakage
risks.description: Fruits are heavy.
risks.mitigation: Use stakes or cages.

common_issues: Powdery mildew; poor pollination
tips: Requires significant space.
Must hand pollinate female flowers using male flowers.

risks.title: Powdery Mildew
risks.description: White spots on massive leaves.
risks.mitigation: Good airflow and prune old leaves.

common_issues: Root rot if water is stagnant
tips: Highly oxygenated water is critical.
Cut leaving stubble for regrowth.

risks.title: Stagnant Water
risks.description: Causes root rot and attracts pests.
risks.mitigation: Use strong air stones.

common_issues: Root rot if water is too warm
tips: Harvest outer leaves for cut-and-come-again yield.
Keep roots below 24C.

risks.title: Root Rot
risks.description: High reservoir temps lead to rot.
risks.mitigation: Keep water cool.

common_issues: Crown rot; slow early growth
tips: Keep basal plate above waterline to avoid crown rot.
Requires patience as it is a slow grower.

risks.title: Crown Rot
risks.description: Base rots if submerged.
risks.mitigation: Keep base dry.

common_issues: Poor pollination without airflow
harvest_indicator: Pods are tender and seeds not bulging
tips: Topping young plants encourages bushier growth.
Shake plants gently to aid pollination indoors.

risks.title: Poor Yield
risks.description: Lack of pollination.
risks.mitigation: Use fans or shake plants.

common_issues: Aphids; blossom drop from heat/stress
tips: Needs high EC during fruiting for heat and yield.
Use heat mat for germination.

risks.title: Blossom Drop
risks.description: Flowers fall off without setting fruit.
risks.mitigation: Ensure stable temp and hand pollinate.

common_issues: Root rot from overwatering
tips: Best started from cuttings.
Prefers slightly drier roots; ensure excellent aeration.

risks.title: Root Rot
risks.description: Roots drown easily.
risks.mitigation: Avoid constant soaking.

common_issues: Slow early growth
harvest_indicator: Leaves are large enough to pick
tips: Resilient but needs consistent nutrients.
Harvest before flowering.

risks.title: Mildew
risks.description: Fungal issues in high humidity.
risks.mitigation: Ensure good airflow.

common_issues: Slow seed germination
tips: Very beginner-friendly.
Responds well to cut-and-come-again.

risks.title: Slow germination
risks.description: Seeds take up to 3 weeks.
risks.mitigation: Soak seeds overnight.

common_issues: Vines breaking under fruit weight; powdery mildew
harvest_indicator: Fruit slips easily from vine; sweet aroma
tips: Crucial to use slings to support fruit.
Needs manual pollination.

risks.title: Vine Stress
risks.description: Heavy fruit breaks vine.
risks.mitigation: Use hammocks/slings for fruit.

common_issues: Bolting in heat; aphids
tips: Heavy feeder, requires high EC.
Keep temperatures low to avoid bitter bolting.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Premature flowering from heat.
risks.mitigation: Keep ambient temps below 24C.

common_issues: Head cracking from uneven watering
harvest_indicator: Head is solid and firm to touch
tips: Requires significant space.
Heavy feeder that depletes water quickly.

risks.title: Head Cracking
risks.description: Sudden influx of water causes splitting.
risks.mitigation: Keep EC and water levels very consistent.

common_issues: Bolting in heat; flea beetles; clubroot
harvest_indicator: Stems pencil-thick, first flower buds appear
tips: Harvest when first flower buds form for tenderest stems.
Cut central stem first then side shoots regrow for second harvest.
Variety Karillo (Rijk Zwaan) tolerates Philippine lowland heat better than imports.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Premature flowering above 26C produces tough fibrous stems.
risks.mitigation: Keep solution below 22C; harvest at first bud sign; provide 30% shade cloth.

risks.title: Flea beetles
risks.description: Tiny holes peppering young leaves.
risks.mitigation: Floating row cover early stage; yellow sticky traps; neem oil weekly.

growth_stages.description: Move to channel when 4 true leaves.

common_issues: BER; cracking on irregular watering; whitefly
source: Rijk Zwaan Solarino datasheet, NoSoilSolutions, Web validation
tips: Plum/paste varieties (e.g. Solarino, San Marzano) have less water content — ideal for sauces.
Maintain steady irrigation to prevent radial cracking.
Drop EC by 0.3 during ripening to concentrate sugars.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot (BER)
risks.description: Plum types especially susceptible due to elongated fruit shape and Ca uptake bottleneck.
risks.mitigation: Stable EC, weekly Cal-Mag supplement, keep humidity 60-70%.

risks.title: Fruit cracking
risks.description: Concentric or radial cracks from sudden EC/water swings.
risks.mitigation: Keep fertigation cycles consistent; avoid drought-then-flood pattern.

common_issues: BER; aphids; thin fruit walls if low Ca
source: Rijk Zwaan Palermo datasheet, IGWorks, NoSoilSolutions
tips: Palermo-type horns ripen faster than blocky bell peppers — earlier red color = sweeter flavor.
Bag clusters with insect mesh during flowering to keep clean fruit.
Pinch first king flower to build stronger bush.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot
risks.description: Elongated fruit prone to Ca starvation at distal tip.
risks.mitigation: Stable EC, weekly Cal-Mag, humidity 60-75%, even fertigation.

risks.title: Aphid colonies
risks.description: Curling new growth, sticky honeydew, sooty mold.
risks.mitigation: Yellow sticky traps; release ladybugs; spray insecticidal soap.

common_issues: Tip burn; bolting in heat; loose leaf bruising
source: Rijk Zwaan Lalique, Howard Resh, Produce Grower
tips: Oakleaf types (e.g. Lalique RZ) hold longer at maturity than romaine — flexible harvest window.
Cut-and-come-again works well — outer leaves regrow 2-3 cycles.
Lobed leaves trap water — boost airflow to avoid mildew.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Brown leaf edges from local Ca shortage.
risks.mitigation: Cap EC at 1.6; keep humidity above 55%; move air across canopy.

risks.title: Bolting
risks.description: Stem elongates and leaves turn bitter above 26C.
risks.mitigation: Hold day temp under 24C; choose bolt-resistant cultivars.

common_issues: Tip burn; pythium in warm reservoir
source: Rijk Zwaan Hydrolique, Howard Resh, ResearchGate
tips: Batavia (summer crisp) tolerates higher temperatures than butterhead — best lettuce for tropical hydroponics.
Hydrolique RZ bred specifically for hydroponic year-round in lowland heat.
Crinkled leaves give better shelf-life — bag whole heads dry.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Inner leaf edges brown from rapid growth.
risks.mitigation: EC <1.8; humidity 55-70%; constant fan over canopy.

risks.title: Pythium root rot
risks.description: Brown slimy roots when reservoir >24C.
risks.mitigation: Chill solution to <22C; sanitize between batches; weekly H2O2 1ml/L.

common_issues: Tip burn; bolting; aphids hide in frills
source: Rijk Zwaan Limambo/Tourbillon, Johnny's Selected Seeds, Howard Resh
tips: Lollo Bionda (green) and Lollo Rossa (red) are visual upgrades for salad mixes — premium price.
Frilly leaves trap aphids — inspect undersides weekly.
Tourbillon RZ stays compact under 28C, ideal for Manila climate.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Browning inside frills from Ca shortfall.
risks.mitigation: EC <1.6; humidity 55-65%; airflow over canopy.

risks.title: Aphid hiding
risks.description: Frills shelter aphid colonies invisible from above.
risks.mitigation: Weekly underside inspection; release ladybugs; neem oil at first sign.

actions: Pack loose to avoid crushing frills

common_issues: Powdery mildew; vine breakage; poor netting from EC swings
harvest_indicator: Full slip from vine, sweet aroma, raised tan netting on rind
source: Rijk Zwaan melon catalog, Dealzer, Agri Farming, NatureTech
tips: Cantaloupe distinct from generic melon — netted rind, orange flesh, slip-free harvest signal.
Maintain even fertigation during fruit sizing or netting will be patchy.
Bump EC to 2.8 last 2 weeks for sugar (Brix) build-up.
Hammock or sling each fruit at golf-ball size — vines snap under weight.

risks.title: Powdery mildew
risks.description: White dust on large leaves during humid spells.
risks.mitigation: Airflow; prune lower leaves; potassium bicarbonate or sulfur spray weekly.

risks.title: Hollow heart / poor pollination
risks.description: Indoor plants get few bees, fruit aborts or hollow-centered.
risks.mitigation: Hand-pollinate male to female flowers AM with brush; keep humidity 55-70%.

risks.title: Vine breakage
risks.description: Heavy fruit (1-2kg) snaps unsupported vines.
risks.mitigation: Mesh slings clipped to overhead trellis line as soon as fruit forms.

growth_stages.description: Sow in warm rockwool or coco plug.

actions: Prune lateral suckers below fruit zone

growth_stages.description: Yellow male and female flowers appear.
Full slip stage — fruit detaches with light pull.
actions: Cure 2-3 days at room temp before refrigerating

common_issues: Tip burn at high EC; aphids in dense rosette
harvest_indicator: One cut at base releases 40-50 uniform baby leaves
source: Rijk Zwaan Salanova/Tourbillon, Johnny's Salanova catalog
tips: Salanova/Multileaf bred so single basal cut yields ready-to-pack baby-leaf mix — labor saver vs hand-picking.
Tourbillon RZ Crispy and Lalique-line Sweet types both fit Salanova workflow.
Premium price point at restaurants — focus on uniform head size for plating.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Inner leaf browning from Ca shortfall.
risks.mitigation: EC <1.6; humidity 55-65%; constant airflow over canopy.

risks.title: Aphids hidden in rosette
risks.description: Dense overlapping leaves shelter colonies.
risks.mitigation: Weekly inspection of rosette base; release ladybugs; neem oil on first sign.

growth_stages.description: Single basal cut releases 40-50 baby leaves.

common_issues: Pods turn fibrous if over-mature; aphids; fruit borer
harvest_indicator: Pods 6-9cm long, snap easily, smooth glossy skin
source: DA-BPI, GrowByCity Philippines, Hydroforia, Oklahoma State Extension
tips: Smooth-pod cultivars (e.g. Smooth Green, Clemson Smooth) lack spines on pods and stems — easier handling, less skin irritation than spiny types.
Harvest every 1-2 days at 6-9cm — pods turn woody fast above 10cm.
Soak seeds 12-24h before sowing to break hard seed coat.
Self-pollinating; no hand-pollination needed but bees boost set indoors.

risks.title: Fibrous overripe pods
risks.description: Pods harden into inedible fiber if missed even by 1-2 days.
risks.mitigation: Daily harvest checks; remove oversized pods to keep plant productive.

risks.title: Aphids and whitefly
risks.description: Sap-suckers on tender new growth and pod undersides.
risks.mitigation: Yellow sticky traps; spray insecticidal soap weekly; release ladybugs.

risks.title: Fruit borer (Earias)
risks.description: Larvae tunnel into pods leaving exit holes.
risks.mitigation: Insect mesh exclusion; pheromone traps; remove infested pods immediately.

growth_stages.description: Soak seeds 12-24h then sow in warm rockwool or coco plug.
Move to Dutch Bucket once 4-5 true leaves.

actions: Wear gloves even with smooth varieties — leaves still mildly irritating

common_issues: BER; cat-facing on cool nights; russet mites; fruit cracking; greenback shoulder
harvest_indicator: Large globe fruit 150-300g, fully colored shoulder-to-blossom-end, slight give when pressed
source: Rijk Zwaan Climberley/Beorange datasheets, UF/IFAS hydroponic tomato guide, Kai farm calibration
tips: Slicer/beefsteak varieties (e.g. Climberley, Beorange, Big Beef, Trust) need strong trellising — fruit reaches 200-400g.
Prune to single leader and remove all suckers weekly; lower yield count but bigger fruit.
Drop nighttime temperature 5-7C below day temp to trigger flowering and improve fruit set.
Hand-pollinate by tapping flower trusses mid-morning when humidity is 60-70%.
Reduce EC by 0.3-0.5 during ripening to push sugar concentration and flavor.
Top the plant once it reaches trellis height — let last 2 trusses ripen, then terminate.
Remove lower leaves below the first ripening truss to improve airflow and reduce fungal pressure.

risks.title: Blossom-end rot (BER)
risks.description: Sunken black lesion at fruit base from localized calcium deficiency — large slicer fruit is highly prone.
risks.mitigation: Hold EC steady (avoid >0.4 swings), supplement Cal-Mag weekly, keep humidity 65-75%, ensure root zone never dries between irrigations.

risks.title: Fruit cracking
risks.description: Concentric or radial splits on shoulder from sudden rehydration after stress or rapid sugar accumulation.
risks.mitigation: Maintain consistent fertigation cycles (4-6 short pulses/day), avoid drought-flood pattern, harvest at breaker stage in rainy season.

risks.title: Cat-facing / zippering
risks.description: Deformed scarred fruit from cold nights (<13C) during flower formation.
risks.mitigation: Keep night temp >=16C, avoid early-season transplants, remove malformed first trusses to redirect energy.

risks.title: Russet mites
risks.description: Microscopic mites bronze the lower stems and leaves; severe infestations collapse the plant.
risks.mitigation: Scout weekly with 10x loupe at stem base, apply sulfur spray at first sign, isolate infected plants immediately.

risks.title: Botrytis (gray mold)
risks.description: Fuzzy gray sporulation on stems, leaves, fruit — favored by humidity >85% and stagnant air.
risks.mitigation: Run circulation fans 24/7, prune lower leaves, vent during cool wet nights, avoid wetting foliage.

growth_stages.description: Sow 1-2 seeds 5mm deep in pre-soaked rockwool plug.

actions: Move to bright light immediately upon emergence

growth_stages.description: True leaves develop, root system establishes.
actions: Pot up to 4-inch block at 6 leaves
Pinch to single seedling per plug

growth_stages.description: Move to Dutch Bucket with perlite/coco mix when first flower truss visible.
actions: Bury stem deep to encourage adventitious roots
Install trellis line and clip stem

growth_stages.description: Rapid vine extension and leaf production.
actions: Train to single leader on twine

growth_stages.description: Trusses with 4-7 yellow flowers form every 3 leaves.
actions: Tap trusses mid-morning to release pollen

growth_stages.description: Green fruit swells; thin to 4-5 fruit per truss for slicer size.
actions: Maintain Ca/K balance, monitor for BER on first trusses

growth_stages.description: Fruit turns from mature green through breaker to full color.
actions: Drop EC to 3.0 to concentrate sugars
Lower-leaf prune up to first ripening truss

growth_stages.description: Continuous pick every 2-3 days as trusses ripen sequentially.
actions: Twist fruit at calyx joint or use pruners
Pick at breaker for shipping, vine-ripe for direct sale
Top plant once trellis reached and let final trusses finish

common_issues: Tip burn at high EC; bolting above 26C; bottom rot in saturated media; aphids in upright cone
harvest_indicator: Tall upright cone-shaped head 25-30cm, dark green outer leaves, firm crisp heart, 200-300g whole-head weight
source: Rijk Zwaan Olmetie RZ datasheet, Cravo greenhouse trial notes, Web validation
tips: Olmetie RZ is a tall single-cut Cos/romaine type bred for hydroponics — uniform upright heads ideal for whole-head packs.
Spacing: 18-22cm head-to-head in NFT/DFT to let outer leaves expand without crowding.
Strong tip-burn tolerance vs older Cos varieties — but still hold EC <=1.6 in hot months.
High resistance to Bremia (downy mildew) races Bl: 16-37EU,39EU and Nasonovia aphid — reduces spray frequency.
Drop water temp to 18-20C with chiller in tropical climate to prevent bolting beyond day 35.
Harvest in early morning when leaves are fully turgid; refrigerate immediately for shelf life.

risks.title: Tip burn
risks.description: Inner leaf margin browns from localized Ca deficiency during rapid growth.
risks.mitigation: Hold EC <=1.6, keep humidity 55-70%, run gentle constant airflow over canopy, supplement Cal-Mag at 0.5g/L weekly.

risks.title: Bolting (premature flowering)
risks.description: Day temps >26C trigger seed-stalk elongation; leaves turn bitter and milky.
risks.mitigation: Use shade cloth 30-40% in dry season, run growlights only 12-14h, harvest by day 40 in hot months.

risks.title: Bottom rot / Pythium
risks.description: Brown collapse at crown from saturated rockwool plus warm root zone.
risks.mitigation: Keep water temp 18-22C, ensure media drains between cycles, sanitize channels between crops with 1% H2O2.

risks.title: Aphids in upright cone
risks.description: Tight vertical leaf overlap shelters Nasonovia and Myzus colonies despite resistance gene.
risks.mitigation: Weekly scouting at heart, release Aphidius wasps preventatively, neem oil drench at first sign.

growth_stages.description: Sow 1 pelleted seed per rockwool plug, 3mm deep.

actions: Move to bright light immediately on emergence

growth_stages.description: First 2-3 true leaves form, root system anchors.
actions: Pinch to single seedling per plug

growth_stages.description: Move to production channel at 4-5 true leaves.
Upright cone-shaped head builds; outer wrap leaves expand.
actions: Top up reservoir daily, monitor pH drift

growth_stages.description: Inner heart firms up into tight upright Cos shape.
actions: Drop EC to 1.3 final week to improve flavor
Increase airflow to prevent inner-leaf moisture
Scout for tip burn and aphids at heart

growth_stages.description: Single cut at base when head reaches 25-30cm tall and 200-300g.
actions: Cut at crown with sanitized blade in early morning
Cool to 4C within 1 hour of cut for max shelf life
